title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Inspector Huishe and the police chief discuss the investigation into the death of Mrs. Agglesden. Huishe notes that the crime scene appeared to be an amateurish attempt at a burglary, possibly staged by Jack Agglesden to divert suspicion. A perplexing detail involves a five-pound note found on Jack, which bore the name of Mrs. Bottley on the back; while Jack claimed it was from his mother, Mr. Agglesden and Gwendolyn Vane asserted that Mrs. Agglesden had refused to give him money that morning. Huishe proposes a theory that an unknown person ("X") overheard an argument, took the money, and tricked Jack into a trap.

The police chief reviews the potential suspects present in the house that night: Jack Agglesden, Gwendolyn Vane, Hester Aigle, the Lindstrom woman, Leo Aigle, and Mary Darlant. Based on a timetable, the police identify a window of opportunity between 6:58 PM and 7:30 PM. They also consider Michael, a brother and automobile salesman, who possessed a key and was previously uninvestigated. Huishe expresses a personal motive to solve the case because he believes Mrs. Agglesden was a good woman.

In a separate setting, Arthur Calhoun (using the name Calgary) observes Moira Craig, the widow of Jacqueline, at a cinema. The following morning, Calhoun visits Moira at her home. Moira discusses the past incident involving Jack, noting that Jack died of pneumonia in prison. She reveals that she had been considering divorcing Jack because of his imprisonment and his lack of stability.
